teachings.teaching: God’s purpose can work through apparent suffering; “the crushed” is aligned with God’s will rather than being meaningless.
Presenting one’s life as an offering for guilt/atonement leads to vindication and lasting effects (God brings “seed” and extended days).
God is ultimately pleased with what is entrusted to His hand; His will will succeed (“will prosper”) through the agent who submits to this purpose.

goals.goal: To affirm that a willing, guilt-bearing suffering leads to God’s approval, resulting in life, future generations, and the successful fulfillment of God’s purpose.
